text: I’ll inspect the fixture manifest, available bomly tooling, and the test harness first, then apply only dependency-related changes and document each vulnerable package in `FIXES.md`.

The repo is a large Maven fixture under `fixtures/bigapp`; `make test` runs a Maven reactor install with tests skipped but test artifacts still built. I’m going to use the bomly scanner next and read the Maven parent/poms that control versions.

The initial scan target was too broad for bomly’s executor, but discovery confirms the Maven projects. I’m narrowing the scan to the reactor parent that `make test` actually builds, then I’ll drill into each reported package before changing versions.

Bomly reports 21 vulnerable packages in the built reactor, with the compact result truncated. I’m writing the full scan JSON to a temporary file so I can enumerate every vulnerability/package instead of relying on the capped summary.

I found the central version properties in `grouper-parent/pom.xml`; several vulnerable transitive packages are best handled by dependency management pins there. I’m generating the full bomly report now, then I’ll make one scoped parent POM edit and verify it closes the audit.

The full JSON command exits nonzero because findings exist, but it produced the report. I’m parsing only `kind == vulnerability` entries now, excluding license policy warnings since the requested scope is vulnerable dependencies.
`jq` is not installed, so I’m using the local scripting runtime only to inspect the JSON report. No repo files are being changed yet.
Ruby is absent too. I’ll use Node if available to extract the package list from the JSON, then proceed with the Maven changes.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const d=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-before.json'); const m=new Map(); for (const f of d.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability')) { const k=f.package.name+'\\t'+f.package.version; m.set(k,(m.get(k)||0)+1)}; for (const [k,v] of [...m].sort()) console.log(v+'\\t'+k)\""

/bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const d=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-before.json'); const rows=[...new Map(d.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id].join('\\t'),f])).values()].sort((a,b)=>(a.package.name+a.id).localeCompare(b.package.name+b.id)); for (const f of rows) console.log([f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.title].join('\\t'))\""

text: There are 21 vulnerable package coordinates in the reactor. Some are direct properties, and others are old transitives from Axis2, AWS SDK, Qpid, Box SDK, Google client, and MySQL; I’m asking bomly for fix context on the remaining packages before pinning.

Most packages have explicit fixed versions. One advisory for `software.amazon.ion:ion-java` is marked by bomly as `not-fixed` even though its affected range says `<1.10.5`; I’ll test a conservative override rather than assume it is unfixable.

I have the fix set now: update direct managed properties where they exist, add dependency-management overrides for vulnerable transitives, and document the three bomly `no-fix-upstream` packages separately. I’m editing only `grouper-parent/pom.xml` and adding `FIXES.md`.

For `ion-java`, I’m going to add an override to a version above the affected range and let bomly decide whether it still flags it. If bomly still reports it as `not-fixed`, `FIXES.md` will say that explicitly.

A few dependencies have explicit versions in child modules, so parent dependency management alone would not affect them. I’m updating those explicit vulnerable coordinates where a fixed version exists, and using exclusions plus the already managed Bouncy Castle `jdk18on` artifacts where the old `jdk15on` artifacts have no fixed release in that artifact line.
The dependency edits are in place. I’m running a Maven build check next; if any override is incompatible or unavailable, I’ll adjust before documenting.
`mvn validate` succeeds. I’m rescanning the reactor now to see exactly what remains after the version changes; no documentation file until the scan result is known.

The rescan reduced vulnerable packages from 21 to 5. The remaining five are all reported by bomly as `no_fix_upstream`, including one remaining `jackson-databind` advisory after the fixed-version upgrade; I’m recording that distinction in `FIXES.md`.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "bomly scan --path fixtures/bigapp/grouper-parent --enrich --audit --format json -o json=/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json >/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.out 2>/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.err; node -e \"const d=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json'); const rows=[...new Map(d.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>[[f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id].join('\\t'),f])).values()].sort((a,b)=>(a.package.name+a.id).localeCompare(b.package.name+b.id)); for (const f of rows) console.log([f.package.name,f.package.version,f.id,f.severity,f.title].join('\\t'))\""

text: I’m adding `FIXES.md` with one package entry for every vulnerable coordinate bomly identified, including the remaining no-fixed-version cases and the reason each changed version/override was chosen.
`FIXES.md` is added. I’m running the required root `make test` now; this is the real build gate for the fixture.

`make test` is still running. It has compiled through the UI and is in the WS module; the checkstyle parser error shown is pre-existing-style output with `failOnViolation=false`, so it has not stopped the build.
`make test` completed successfully. I’m doing a final status check to make sure only the remediation files changed and no build artifacts were left as tracked edits.

tool: /bin/bash -lc "node -e \"const before=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-before.json'); const after=require('/tmp/bomly-grouper-after.json'); const b=new Set(before.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>f.package.name)); const a=new Set(after.findings.filter(f=>f.kind==='vulnerability').map(f=>f.package.name)); console.log('before vulnerable packages:', b.size); console.log([...b].sort().join('\\n')); console.log('after vulnerable packages:', a.size); console.log([...a].sort().join('\\n'))\""
